reasoning: The informal statement says ℤ/nℤ is not a group under multiplication for n > 1. The Lean formalization asserts ¬(∀ x : ZMod n, ∃ y, x*y = 1 ∧ y*x = 1), i.e., not every element has a two-sided multiplicative inverse. Since ZMod n with multiplication is automatically a (commutative) monoid — associativity and the identity 1 hold in Mathlib's CommRing instance — the only group axiom that can fail is existence of inverses. Hence "ZMod n is not a group under ·" is logically equivalent to "not every element has a multiplicative inverse", which is exactly what the Lean statement says. The hypothesis n > 1 is preserved, and both quantifier structures match. This is a faithful formalization.
